projects.title: Algorithmic Trading AI System
projects.description: Production trading estate proving governance, model-promotion controls, execution reliability, and observability under live conditions.
projects.longDescription: Built to answer a hard operational question: how do you run research-heavy model development without letting fragile models leak into live execution? This trading estate separates experimentation from production execution, enforces promotion gates through shared contracts, and keeps live operations observable in real time. Underneath, the platform spans three interconnected repositories (tf_1, Trading-Win, Trading-WSL). The ML engine trains four model categories: hourly macro-regime classifiers (5-class directional labels STRONG_SELL→STRONG_BUY), high-frequency seconds-bar models with 30-minute lookback windows, multi-task L2 order book models predicting direction + bid-ask spread transitions + market impact regression, and a pre-market gap-opener ranker scoring probability of intraday moves from float, short ratio, relative volume, and news sentiment. Models are gated through a JSON-Logic promotion pipeline enforced via a shared ml-contracts schema repository (git submodule). The execution layer manages headless IBKR Gateway sessions (paper + live), appends market data to Parquet manifests, and consumes only models tagged production.alias. A shared FastAPI notification microservice (TelegramNotifications) delivers real-time alerts across the estate.
projects.businessRelevance: Governance, promotion control, execution reliability, and observability for high-stakes automated decisions.

metrics.models: 4 model categories: hourly, seconds, L2 order book (multi-task), gap opener
metrics.infrastructure: 3-repo estate with shared contract governance via ml-contracts submodule
metrics.dataIngestion: Databento L2 order book + IBKR historical bars in append-only Parquet manifests

highlights: Multi-task L2 order book model: direction + spread transition + market impact in one network
JSON-Logic promotion gates enforcing schema contracts before any model reaches production
Headless IBKR Gateway management — paper and live modes with smart port probing
Pre-market gap-opener ranker using float, short ratio, relative volume, and news sentiment
Shared FastAPI microservice for real-time Telegram notifications across the estate
Full CI pipeline with cross-smoke testing between Windows and WSL environments

detailPage.heroSummary: An end-to-end trading estate that separates ML research from execution, governs model promotion through shared contracts, and keeps production trading infrastructure observable in real time.
detailPage.schemaDescription: A full-stack algorithmic trading ecosystem spanning TensorFlow signal generation, IBKR execution, Databento market data ingestion, JSON-Logic promotion gates, and FastAPI notifications.

projects.title: AI Memoir & Narrative Pipeline
projects.description: Document-intelligence workflow proving retrieval quality, editorial control, and auditability in long-form decision content.
projects.longDescription: A deterministic long-form document pipeline that transforms fragmented source material into a structured narrative output. The workflow combines semantic retrieval, structured patch generation, editorial quality gates, and audit-friendly run tracking so changes remain controlled, explainable, and reversible. The architecture demonstrates how LLM systems can be used in production when generation is constrained by retrieval quality, validation rules, and explicit approval points.
projects.businessRelevance: Document intelligence, retrieval quality, editorial control, and audit-friendly workflow design.

metrics.pipeline: Ingest → Propose → Patch → Quality Gate → DOCX export (fully deterministic)
metrics.scale: Processes multi-year email and chat archives into structured narrative
metrics.quality: Narrative safeguards: structural anchors, agency beats, tension calibration on every run
metrics.observability: W&B-style run tracking with per-run metrics and full CHANGELOG audit trail

highlights: FAISS-powered semantic retrieval over fragmented long-form source material
LLM-backed proposal generation with atomic chapter patch application
Narrative safeguards enforced on every pipeline run — not just at export
Editorial approval workflow with quality-gate CI preventing regressions
Full observability: unique run IDs, metrics logs, and CHANGELOG audit trail
Shared TelegramNotifications microservice for real-time pipeline alerts

detailPage.metaDescription: Detailed case study of Jason Rae's AI Narrative Pipeline covering deterministic chapter generation, FAISS retrieval, editorial quality gates, and narrative safeguards.
detailPage.metaKeywords: AI Memoir Pipeline, LLM Workflow Case Study, FAISS Retrieval, Narrative Pipeline, Document Intelligence
detailPage.heroSummary: A deterministic LLM pipeline that transforms fragmented long-form source material into publication-ready output while preserving retrieval quality, editorial control, and full auditability.
detailPage.schemaDescription: A deterministic LLM narrative pipeline that ingests long-form source material, retrieves relevant context semantically, applies structured chapter patches, and validates output quality before export.

projects.title: AI Desktop Agent Orchestrator
projects.description: Workflow orchestration framework proving process control, QA scoring, productivity measurement, and cost observability across autonomous agents.
projects.longDescription: A multi-desktop Python automation framework that manages AI coding agents (VS Code Copilot) running across multiple virtual desktops simultaneously. OpenAI Computer Use API provides visual detection of approval buttons (Allow, Keep Edits, Try Again) for fully autonomous operation. When no approval fires within 1 hour, the orchestrator calls OpenAI to auto-generate 10 fresh coding prompts drawn from the live project's documentation, then distributes them round-robin across active panels. A transcript quality dashboard scores each panel's output (COMPLETED / IN_PROGRESS / NEEDS_REVISION / BLOCKED), maintains a feedback blacklist for failed prompts, and tracks all OpenAI API costs per session. Includes a master CLI launcher, pytest coverage, and full CI documentation.
projects.businessRelevance: Agent orchestration, process control, QA scoring, productivity measurement, and cost observability.

metrics.scale: Manages multiple AI agents across simultaneous virtual desktops
metrics.automation: Zero human intervention — automatic prompt generation and agent restarts
metrics.quality: 4-tier transcript scoring: COMPLETED / IN_PROGRESS / NEEDS_REVISION / BLOCKED
metrics.costTracking: Real-time OpenAI API cost tracking per run and per agent

highlights: OpenAI Computer Use API for visual button detection — no brittle pixel coordinates
Round-robin prompt distribution across simultaneous Copilot agent panels
Auto-generates fresh prompts from live project docs when agents go idle for 1 hour
Transcript quality pipeline with blacklist feedback loop to avoid bad prompts
Real-time API cost dashboard per agent and per session
Installable Python package with pip install -e.[dev] and full pytest suite

detailPage.metaDescription: Detailed case study of Jason Rae's AI Desktop Agent Orchestrator covering autonomous multi-desktop agent control, prompt generation, transcript scoring, and feedback-loop automation.
detailPage.metaKeywords: AI Agent Orchestrator, Desktop Automation, Computer Use API, Virtual Desktops, Python Automation
detailPage.heroSummary: A multi-desktop orchestration framework that keeps several AI coding agents productive at once by detecting approvals visually, generating prompts when work stalls, and continuously scoring output quality.
detailPage.schemaDescription: A Python automation framework that orchestrates multiple AI coding agents across virtual desktops, monitors approval states visually, generates prompts from project docs, and scores transcript quality.

projects.title: Multilingual Travel Authorization SaaS
projects.description: Compliance-first multilingual travel authorization SaaS proving regulated workflow design, policy monitoring, and full-stack delivery across payments, auth, and compliance.
projects.longDescription: A full-stack SaaS application guiding travelers through government travel authorization applications in multiple languages. Built as a Turborepo monorepo with separate packages for UI components, validators (Zod schemas), email templates (React Email), content, and an automated policy monitor. Wizard-based flows support family and group applications. A real-time compliance framework ensures fee transparency. The monitoring package scrapes government websites on a Vercel cron schedule and runs OpenAI/Anthropic semantic analysis to detect policy changes before travelers are affected. Authentication via Clerk, payments via Stripe, PostgreSQL managed by Prisma. E2E testing with Playwright, accessibility validated by axe-core.
projects.businessRelevance: Compliance workflows, policy monitoring, full-stack delivery, payments, authentication, accessibility, and production testing discipline.

metrics.architecture: Turborepo monorepo — 5 packages: ui, validators, content, emails, monitor
metrics.compliance: LLM-powered monitoring of government policy changes via cron + OpenAI/Anthropic
metrics.stack: Full SaaS: auth, payments, DB, email, E2E tests, and a11y validation baked in

highlights: LLM-powered automated scraping + analysis of government sites for policy change detection
Wizard-based multi-step application flows with family and group support
Full fee transparency compliance framework (government vs. service fee breakdown)
Shared Zod validator packages ensuring consistent data contracts across the monorepo
React Email templates for all transactional and notification emails
Clerk + Stripe + Prisma + Vercel — fully integrated production SaaS stack

detailPage.metaDescription: Detailed case study of Jason Rae's Multilingual Travel Authorization SaaS covering compliance monitoring, full-stack platform architecture, payments, authentication, and accessibility validation.
detailPage.metaKeywords: Travel Authorization SaaS, Next.js Case Study, Compliance Monitoring, Stripe SaaS, Multilingual Product
detailPage.heroSummary: A production-ready SaaS platform for guided travel authorization applications that combines multilingual UX, fee transparency, authentication, payments, and LLM-powered policy monitoring.

caseStudies.title: Forecast Governance Reset
caseStudies.description: Public-safe engagement summary: forecasts were overstated or inconsistent because CRM behavior, stage discipline, and accountability varied across markets.
highlights: Diagnosed governance and behavior root causes before modeling
Improved review cadence and sales-cycle assumptions
Helped leadership move toward stronger forecast confidence

caseStudies.title: Commercial Analytics Foundation
caseStudies.description: Public-safe engagement summary from a broader foundation-fix brief: Sales, Finance, and leadership lacked one trusted view across revenue, margin, pricing, and opportunity data.
highlights: Standardized KPI definitions and improved reporting ownership
Supported centralized Power BI model and governance direction
Reduced conflicting narratives and improved decision speed

caseStudies.title: Sales Engine From Zero
caseStudies.description: A new market required a functioning commercial engine under severe operating constraints.
highlights: Built targeting, distributor identification, and operating rhythm from scratch
Connected process, prioritization, and analytics to execution discipline
Delivered major growth improvement against the original target

caseStudies.title: Margin And Pricing Logic Repair
caseStudies.description: Pricing, FX, cost, rebate, and incentive logic distorted how leadership interpreted commercial performance.
highlights: Rebuilt decomposition logic and aligned methodology
Identified defects creating margin distortion across markets
Improved pricing and margin interpretation for leadership

caseStudies.title: Applied AI Enablement
caseStudies.description: Leaders wanted faster analysis and workflow leverage, but teams lacked shared language, governance, and practical use cases tied to real commercial decisions.
highlights: Built training and enablement around practical business use cases
Connected governance, operating model, and adoption rather than hype
Positioned AI as a decision-system capability, not a side experiment
